# Client Portal SvelteKit LLM Guide

Purpose: guide agents implementing or customizing the `client-portal-sveltekit` template.

Template ID: client-portal-sveltekit
Status: ready (local baseline)
Framework: sveltekit
Required modules: auth, identity, email, gateway, customer, invoice, file-media, storage-entitlements, audit-log
Check: pnpm check:spec

## Read First

- `microservices.template.json`
- `microservices.config.json`
- `microservices.lock.json`
- `README.agent.md`
- `docs/api-boundary.md`

## What It Is

An auth-gated client portal for professional-services businesses. A logged-in
customer sees only their own invoices and documents and their account; staff get
an admin side spanning all customers, invoices, and activity.

## Architecture Rule

SvelteKit route files (`+page.server.ts`, `+server.ts`) are adapters. Domain/API
logic lives in detached module use cases.

```txt
src/routes/portal/invoices/+page.server.ts
  -> parse session + call listInvoices (customerId-scoped)

src/routes/portal/files/+page.server.ts
  -> owner-scoped listFiles + quota reservation/release + two-step upload: createUploadTicket -> PUT bytes -> completeUpload

modules/invoice/src/use-cases/*.ts        -> billing behavior
modules/file-media/src/use-cases/*.ts     -> document/file behavior
modules/storage-entitlements/src/service  -> storage quota, packages, share links
modules/customer/src/use-cases/*.ts       -> account behavior
modules/audit-log/src/use-cases/*.ts      -> activity trail
modules/identity/...                      -> login codes / sessions
modules/email/...                         -> login-code delivery
```

Dependencies (repositories/stores) are created in `src/hooks.server.ts` and
injected through `event.locals`. D1/R2 adapters are used when bindings exist;
in-memory adapters back local dev and are seeded by `src/lib/server/demo.ts`.

## Scoping Rule

Customer sessions are scoped to their own `customerId`. Portal routes pass
`customerId` to `listInvoices` and verify ownership before opening a record, so
existence never leaks across customers. File routes pass `customerId` as
`ownerId` to file-media and storage-entitlements. Staff routes are gated on
`role === "staff"`.

## Approval Gates

Require user approval before changing:

- secrets
- remote migrations
- webhooks
- payment/email provider behavior
- Cloudflare resources
- remote deployments
- forked module internals

## Local Baseline

```bash
pnpm microservices check --json
pnpm microservices local setup
pnpm dev
```

Passwordless login is backed by `@microservices-sh/identity` and
`@microservices-sh/email`; the dev role cookie remains local-only for demos.
Before beta, broaden D1/R2 migration coverage for invoice, customer, and
audit-log persistence.
